message.thinking:

We have text: It gives a list of sections. It describes contents. Let's analyze.

Question 1: "Chapters" - Does the text list the main sections of the thesis? The text includes "Bakalaura darbs sastāv no četrām nodaļām:" then enumerates 1-4. So yes. Feedback: It lists four main chapters. So complies true.

Question 2: "Description" - Does the text briefly describe contents of each section? The text indeed gives description for each chapter. It mentions theoretical materials, modern solutions, practical solution implementation, methodology. So yes. Feedback: yes. Complies true.

Question 3: "Structure" - Can it be easily understood what is the general structure of the thesis just from this text? The text outlines four chapters. The overall structure: introduction? Actually the text includes a "Pētījuma metožu apraksts" at top, but not mention of introduction? It seems it's the final part of introduction? Actually the final text appears to be a part of introduction describing methods and chapters. The structure of thesis can be gleaned: 1) theoretical, 2) review of solutions, 3) practical implementation, 4) conclusion with methodology. So yes. So compliance true.

But check if there is any missing part: maybe the thesis also includes abstract, background etc. But from this text, the structure is basically 4 chapters. The question: "Can it be easily understood what is the general structure of the thesis just from this text?" Yes. So compliance true.

Thus produce JSON array with 3 objects.

We must not add any other output. So produce JSON array.
